senses.gloss: The nineteenth letter of the Arabic alphabet, written in the Arabic script; preceded by ع (ʕ) and followed by ف (f).
The twenty-eighth and last numeral symbol of the Arabic alphabet (traditional abjad order, used for list numbering), written in the Arabic script; preceded by ظ (ẓ).

etymology.text: Derived from ع (ʕ), and originally written as such (in rasm) by analogy with Nabatean tradition, as Nabatean cognates of Arabic words with /ɣ/ were indiscriminately spelled using 𐢗 (ʿ).

Habib Anthony Salmone, An Advanced Learner's Arabic-English Dictionary (1889)
entries.lexicon_ar: حبيب سالمون، قاموس عربي انجليزي متقدم للمتعلمين
entries.body_html:
غ
غ
a. Ghain . The nineteenth letter of the alphabet. Its numerical value is One
Thousand, (1000).

غ: الغين من الحروف الحَلْقِيّة ومخرجها من الحلق، وهي أَيضاً من الحروف

المَجْهُورةِ، والغينُ والخاء في حيز واحد.

entries.lexicon_en: Murtaḍa al-Zabīdī, Tāj al-ʿArūs fī Jawāhir al-Qamūs (d. 1790 CE)
entries.body_html: من كتاب الْقَامُوس فِي اللِّسَان: الْعين من الْحُرُوف الحلقية وأبضا من الْحُرُوف المجهورة وَهِي وَالْخَاء فِي حيّز وَاحِد. قَالَ شَيخنَا: أبدلت من خرفين: من الْخَاء الْمُهْملَة فِي قَوْلهم: غطر بِيَدِهِ يعطر، بمعني خطر يخْطر، حَكَاهُ ابْن جني وَجَمَاعَة، وَمن الْعين الْمُهْملَة فِي قَوْلهم: لغن فِي لعن، قَالَه ايْنَ أم قَاسم وَغَيره 2

entries.lexicon_en: Arabic-English Lexicon by Edward William Lane (d. 1876)
entries.lexicon_ar: المعجم العربي الإنجليزي لإدوارد وليام لين
entries.body_html:
غ alphabetical letter غ

The nineteenth letter of the alphabet: called غَيْنٌ.

It is one of the letters termed حَلْقِيَّة [or faucial], (L, TA,) its place of utterance being the upper part of the fauces, near to that of ح, (TA in art. غين,) the same place as that of خ, (L, TA,) [from which it differs in being pronounced with the voice, for] it is of the letters termed مَجْهُورَة

[or vocal], (L, TA, and K in art. غين,) and of those that are termed مُسْتَعْلِيَة [q. v.]; one should not reiterate the voice in uttering it, so as to exceed what is right, nor neglect exactness in respect of its place of utterance, so as to render it obscure, but should make it thoroughly distinct, and clear: it is not an augmentative letter: and [it is said that] it is not substituted [for another letter]: (K in art. غين:) [but this is a mistake; for] it is substituted for two letters; for خ, in غَطَرَ بِيَدِهِ, aor. ـْ meaning خَطَرَ, aor. ـْ mentioned by IJ and several others, (MF, TA,) and in غَطَرَ بِذَنَبِهِ for خَطَرَ; (TA in art. خطر;) and for ع, in لَغَنَّ for لَعَنَّ, mentioned by Ibn-Umm- Kásim and others, (MF, TA,) [and in نَشُوغٌ for نَشُوغٌ,] and in اِرْمَغَلَّ for اِرْمَعَلَّ, and also in الغَيْنُ as signifying “ thirst ” and “ the clouds. ” (TA in art. غين.)

A2: [As a numeral, it denotes A thousand.]
